techniques.evidence_quote: Exposed IAM Access keys in Public S3

techniques.technique_name: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API
techniques.evidence_quote: Exposed IAM Access keys in Public S3
Request via AWS CLI to 'Start Instance' & 'RequestServiceQuotaIncrease'
Via CLI New IAM Resource created, assigned additional permissions & New microservice deployed
Two new IAM Resource created & assigned additional permissions and roles for SES & SQS (AttachUserPolicy)
SES commands issued to verify new domain, DKIM and email address
Delete profile elements, policies and access keys for a previously created user, executed from external IP not owned by customer
Increase Sending Quote initiated & Sending Rate, and SNS quota
Sending an abnormally large amount of emails in mass phishing campaign

attack_chain.technique_name: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API
attack_chain.description: Attacker discovered plaintext IAM access keys stored in a publicly accessible S3 bucket, providing initial foothold credentials.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Exposed IAM Access keys in Public S3

attack_chain.description: Attacker used the exfiltrated IAM access keys via AWS CLI to authenticate and begin interacting with the AWS environment, earliest evidence dated March 29th 2024.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Earliest evidence of compromise March 29th, 2024. Exposed IAM Access keys in Public S3 Request via AWS CLI to 'Start Instance' & 'RequestServiceQuotaIncrease'

attack_chain.description: Attacker issued CLI commands to start EC2 instances and request service quota increases, probing the environment's capabilities and limits.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Request via AWS CLI to 'Start Instance' & 'RequestServiceQuotaIncrease'

attack_chain.description: Attacker created new IAM users via CLI, assigned them additional permissions, and deployed a new microservice to establish persistent access and expand capabilities.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Via CLI New IAM Resource created, assigned additional permissions & New microservice deployed. Increase Sending Quote initiated & Sending Rate, and SNS quota

attack_chain.description: Attacker created two additional IAM users, attaching policies granting SES and SQS permissions using AttachUserPolicy to enable mass email sending.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Two new IAM Resource created & assigned additional permissions and roles for SES & SQS (AttachUserPolicy)

attack_chain.description: Attacker used SES API commands to verify a new sending domain, configure DKIM, and verify email addresses, preparing the infrastructure for a phishing campaign.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: SES commands issued to verify new domain, DKIM and email address

attack_chain.description: Attacker deleted login profile elements, attached policies, and access keys for a previously created IAM user from an external IP not owned by the customer, covering tracks.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Delete profile elements, policies and access keys for a previously created user, executed from external IP not owned by customer

attack_chain.description: Attacker abused SES to send an abnormally large volume of emails as part of a mass phishing campaign, exhausting sending quotas and triggering AWS bounce rate alerts.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Sending an abnormally large amount of emails in mass phishing campaign. Customer contacted by AWS re SES email bounce rate issue and rate increase

initial_access_vector: Plaintext IAM access keys discovered in a publicly accessible S3 bucket
persistence_mechanism: Creation of multiple new IAM users with attached policies granting SES, SQS, and SNS permissions via AttachUserPolicy
privilege_escalation: AttachUserPolicy used to assign additional IAM permissions and roles for SES and SQS to newly created IAM users
